hero.question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd has essentially priced this into the noise at 5.3%, treating a three-country strike as a tail risk event that requires escalation beyond even the worst regional scenarios of recent years. The bar for this is absurdly high, you'd need not just another major confrontation but sustained strikes across three distinct nations, and the market is right to treat that as unlikely given current trajectory and Israeli strategic doctrine.

daily_take.headline: Israel Strikes Market Capitulates on Multi-Country Escalation Thesis
daily_take.deck: The Israel 3-country strike market just dumped 6.5 points to 5.3%, signaling traders are pricing out a major regional escalation in 2026. The move feels reactive to recent geopolitical de-escalation noise rather than fundamental shift in risk. At 5.3%, this is oversold. A smart bettor waits for stabilization then fades the capitulation.

sidebar.headline: Astros got smacked down 7.5 points in 24 hours, the kind of panic selling that happens when sharp money moves first. Check the live betting action before following.
sidebar.label: Astros ALCS: 6.5%

sidebar.headline: Iran regime recognition stuck flat at 17.5% on minimal volume. The real move happens when Trump's policy team signals intent, not before. Dust until then.
